title: The Okoye Commission
summary: Marcus Hale, a meticulous Brooklyn contractor, finishes a perfect kitchen for absent Upper East Side clients and feels the hollow of building beautiful things for people who will never notice. When his tile-setter friend Ray calls about a professor in Crown Heights who has exquisite taste, a detailed spec, and no cash, Marcus visits the brownstone on Sterling Place and encounters Nadia Okoye — an economist who proposes equity participation instead of payment. The brownstone, the spec, and the fig tree in the garden convince him to say yes to the strangest deal of his career.
themes: the gap between price and worth
continuity_notes: Marcus has agreed to the deal. Start of November build. Elena is 13, sharp, her mother's math brain. Danny and Sofia established as shop characters. The Sheffield kitchen exists as a contrast object. Marcus's father referenced but not seen — retired deck builder in Bensonhurst. Nadia's mother died 2 years ago, was Nigerian-trained engineer. Brownstone built ~1895.
